english: and mčér-pa-żo-śa (mcher pa zho sha) are mentioned. — 3. toll (?), pay (?), żo-śás °tsó-ba (zho shas 'tsho ba) Tar., Stg. a publican Cs., a soldier Schr., prob. any officer that receives salary or pay.
Med., żóg-ka (zhog ka) Sch., = sṅá-dro (snga dro) morning, fore-noon; żógs-ja (zhogs ja) tea at breakfast Cs.
lower, nether, żoṅ-kań-pa (zhong khang pa) the lower part of the house, żoṅ-rtsé (zhong rtse) the lower and the upper part; żoṅ-żóṅ (zhong zhong) deepened, excavated, hollow, uneven C.

1. the original meaning of the word is yet uncertain; at present used in C.: *żo' dé-wa* (zhod bde ba), *żo-jág* (zho 'jag)*, peace, quietness, tranquillity, *kho żo'-dé-la mi żay* (kho zhod bde la mi bzhag) he gives him no rest, causes him much trouble; *sém-kyi żo' dé-mo* (sems kyi zhod bde mo) peace of mind, evenness of temper; *żo'* (zhod) or *żo' dé-mo* (zhod bde mo) or jám-pa ('jam pa) gentleness, meekness. - 2. Sch.: high-water, floods, inundations Wts., C.; sṅa taṅ pyi żod (snga tang phyi zhod) first drought, then inundation Wdk. - 3. udder W., C.

resp. °číb-pa (chibs pa) to mount, c. la; rtá-la żón-pa (rta la zhon pa) to ride, on horseback, śiṅ-rta-la (shing rta la) to ride, in a carriage, frq.; rtá-la żón-nas lhó-pyogs-su °gró-ba (rta la zhon nas lho phyogs su 'gro ba) to ride southward, to travel on horseback towards the south S.g.; also c. accus.: bżón-pa żón-pa (bzhon pa zhon pa) to mount a horse or a carriage Lex.; żón-du jùg-pa (zhon du 'jug pa) (= skyón-pa (skyon pa)) to let mount.
= jóms-pa ('joms pa)? rgas żóm-ste (rgas zhom ste) weighed down by old age Sch.; cf. ɣżóm-pa (gzhom pa).

1. żol-ɣyág (zhol g.yag), yak-bull, Bos grunniens Sch.; rá-ma żól-mo (ra ma zhol mo) a long-haired goat Mil. nt. - 2. village belonging to a convent Mil., so Shikatse is the sde-żól (sde zhol) of Tashilhunpo. - 3. postp., under, Sch. (cf ɣżól-ba (gzhol ba) II).
Sch.: 1. to sport, joke, play, sing, (cf. ga-ɣża (ga gzha)). - 2. to believe, trust, confide.
earlier form for ja-tsón ('ja' tshon) rain-bow.

1. anus Med., ɣżaṅ-ka (gzhang ka) id.; ɣżaṅ-nád (gzhang nad), ɣżaṅ-°brúm (gzhang 'brum) piles, hemorrhoids Med.; ɣżaṅ-srín (gzhang srin) a kind of intestinal worms Lt. - 2. privy parts, *żaṅ-°ág* (zhang 'ag) W. catamenial blood; *żaṅ-tsoṅ-ma* (zhang tshong ma) W. = smád-tsoṅ-ma (smad tshong ma); *żaṅ-stoṅ* (zhang stong) W., without breeches, with a bare posterior.

(the last esp. in W.), 1. adj. and sbst., other, the other, another, żan mi (zhan mi) the other men Dzl., ɣżán-pas lhág-par (gzhan pas lhag par) more than others Dzl.; slób-ma ɣżán-dag (slob ma gzhan dag) the other scholars Dzl.; mtsan ɣżán-pa (mtshan gzhan pa) the other signs Dzl.; blón-po ɣżán-ma-rnams (blon po gzhan ma rnams) the other ministers Glr.; bú-mo ɣżán-pas čé-rgyu med (bu mo gzhan pas che rgyu med) she is not taller than the other girls (pas = las, not from pa) Glr.; ɣżan rgol ma nus (gzhan rgol ma nus) others were not able to resist them (= nobody could do them any harm) Glr.; ɣżán-du (gzhan du) to some other place, °gró-ba ('gro ba) to go (to some other place) = to go away, to start; elsewhere; in another way, v. example °dzin-pa ('dzin pa) l, 4; also: ɣżán-du ma sems śig (gzhan du ma sems shig) Dzl., suppose or believe nothing else, do not think that the matter can be otherwise, frq. used like our 'of course'; ɣżán-na (gzhan na) elsewhere; ɣżán-nas (gzhan nas) from some other place; ɣżán-nas °grúb-tu med (gzhan nas 'grub tu med) it cannot be accomplished from any other quarter, by any body else Mil. - 2. adv. otherwise, else, on the other hand W.; ɣżán-yaṅ (gzhan yang) further, furthermore, or else, (just) to mention some other circumstance, frq. - ɣżan-bsgrúb (gzhan bsgrub) Lex. seems to be some logical term Gram. - ɣżan-°prúl (gzhan 'phrul) n. of a heaven inhabited by certain gods Glr., Mil. - ɣżan-dbáṅ (gzhan dbang) dependent on others Was, cf. raṅ-dbáṅ (rang dbang).
